# Stackmap

> Lightweight architecture mapping for social purpose organisations.

Stackmap is a free, open source, browser-based tool that helps charities, social enterprises, councils, and cooperatives map their technology architecture. Users walk through a guided wizard to document their organisational functions, software systems, services, data categories, integrations, and ownership — then export diagrams, cost breakdowns, and risk assessments.

## Key facts

- Runs entirely in the browser (localStorage). No account, no server, no data leaves the device.
- Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Mermaid.js for diagrams.
- Includes TechFreedom risk scoring across five dimensions: jurisdiction, continuity, surveillance, lock-in, and cost exposure.
- Supports import from JSON (full architecture) and CSV (systems list with fuzzy matching).
- Designed for non-technical users — plain language, no TOGAF or ArchiMate.

## Who it's for

- Small charities with no IT team
- Social enterprises managing growth
- Councils planning LGR transitions
- Cooperatives and community organisations
- Anyone who thinks "we should probably document this"

## Links

- Website: https://stackmap.org
- Source: https://github.com/dataforaction-tom/stackmap
- TechFreedom programme: https://techfreedom.eu
